SenderNotAllowedToUseHandle(bytes32,addr.notice: Returned if the sender is not allowed to use the handle.

ZamaProtocolUnsupported().notice: Returned if the Zama protocol is not supported on the current chain

MARGIN_GOLD().notice: Margin over the approval line for gold.

MARGIN_SILVER().notice: Margin (in score fixed-point units) over the approval line for silver.

authorizeLender(address).notice: Grant a lending protocol permission over the caller's verdict AND         tier. Never the score, never the features.

userdoc.notice: The first confidential AI credit oracle on Ethereum. v2 adds encrypted RISK TIERS on top of the binary verdict:   tier 0: rejected   tier 1: approved, low margin      (bronze)   tier 2: approved, solid margin    (silver)   tier 3: approved, strong margin   (gold) The tier is computed under FHE from the score margin and stored encrypted. A lender the user authorizes can consume the TIER without ever learning the underlying score, and certainly never the raw features. Privacy is graded: the world sees ciphertexts, an authorized lender sees a coarse tier, only the applicant can see the score, and nobody ever sees the inputs. Scoring (all under FHE, unsigned split-weight form):   posSum = bias + sum(posW[i] * f[i])   negSum = threshold + sum(negW[i] * f[i])   approved = posSum >= negSum   margin thresholds: tier2 at negSum + MARGIN_SILVER, tier3 at negSum + MARGIN_GOLD
